article_title: Skyroot Aerospace Becomes India's First Space-Tech Unicorn With $60 Million Round Ahead of Vikram-1 Maiden Orbital Launch

content_preview.title: Skyroot Aerospace Becomes India's First Space-Tech Unicorn With $60 Million Round Ahead of Vikram-1 Maiden Orbital Launch
content_preview.summary: Hyderabad-based Skyroot raised $60 million co-led by GIC and Sherpalo Ventures at a $1.1 billion valuation, with Vikram-1's first orbital flight targeted for June 2026.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Skyroot Aerospace, the Hyderabad-based launch startup founded by two former ISRO engineers, has raised $60 million in a round that values the company at $1.1 billion, making it India's first space-tech unicorn, [according to TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/07/indias-first-space-tech-unicorn-emerges-as-skyroot-gears-up-for-orbital-launch/). The capital arrives weeks before the planned maiden orbital flight of Vikram-1, which Skyroot has positioned as India's first private ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight 480-word News piece with clean separation of funding mechanics, vehicle specs, and explicit hedging on undisclosed details (specific launch date, customer manifest, cap table).
editor_notes.source_verification: Read both snapshots from disk. TechCrunch (source-0) confirms verbatim: $60M total / $50M primary equity co-led by GIC and Sherpalo / $10M structured debt from BlackRock-affiliated funds, $1.1B pre-money valuation, doubling 2023's $500M mark, founded 2018 by ex-ISRO engineers Pawan Kumar Chandana and Naga Bharath Daka, Vikram-1 350 kg LEO payload, Sriharikota spaceport, 'targeting a June launch', Vikram-2 one-ton-class cryogenic-stage 2027 debut, Greenko founders as additional investors. Tribune (source-1) confirms verbatim: Hyderabad headquarters with Max-Q and Infinity campuses, carbon composite + 3D-printed propulsion construction, Ram Shriram (Sherpalo founder, Alphabet board member) joining Skyroot's board, Playbook Partners / Arkam Ventures / Shanghvi Family Office investor list. Chandana quote 'India's first private orbital rocket, marking a significant milestone both for India and the global space sector' verified verbatim in Tribune.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific traces to its cited authority. Multi-source corroboration on company background, founders, and Vikram-1/Vikram-2 specs.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and informational. Forward-looking statements properly attributed to company.
editor_notes.originality: First-of-cluster review for Skyroot funding announcement (PR #1198 covers same topic and will be rejected as duplicate downstream).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean APPROVE. Multi-sourced, well-attributed funding-announcement piece.
